title: abelmoschus rostellatus
intro: Abelmoschus rostellatus, also known as the pointed gourd or pointed calabash, is a plant species in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Abelmoschus rostellatus include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its herbal remedies, and its seeds and leaves are used in various preparations, including tea, oil, and powder.
